title: Redditch United F.C.

[Southern League](/wiki/Southern_Football_League "Southern Football League") Premier Division Central, 18th of 22

is an English [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club based in [Redditch](/wiki/Redditch "Redditch"), Worcestershire. The club participates in the [Southern League](/wiki/Southern_Football_League "Southern Football League") Premier Division Central and play their home games at the Valley Stadium.

and joined the [Birmingham and District League](/wiki/West_Midlands_(Regional)_League "West Midlands (Regional) League") in 1921. After the war, they played in the [Birmingham Combination](/wiki/Birmingham_Combination "Birmingham Combination"), and were champions in 1953, before returning to the Birmingham and District.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)

**Redditch United Football Club** is an English [football](/wiki/Association_football "Association football") club based in [Redditch](/wiki/Redditch "Redditch"), Worcestershire. The club participates in the [Southern League](/wiki/Southern_Football_League "Southern Football League") Premier Division Central and play their home games at the Valley Stadium.

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Redditch_United_F.C.&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

They were originally called **Redditch** and joined the [Birmingham and District League](/wiki/West_Midlands_(Regional)_League "West Midlands (Regional) League") in 1921. After the war, they played in the [Birmingham Combination](/wiki/Birmingham_Combination "Birmingham Combination"), and were champions in 1953, before returning to the Birmingham and District.[[1]](#cite_note-:0-1)

In 1971, they changed their name to Redditch United and the following season joined the [Southern League](/wiki/Southern_Football_League "Southern Football League"). In [1975–76](/wiki/1975%E2%80%9376_Southern_Football_League "1975–76 Southern Football League"), they won Division One North and were promoted to the Premier Division. In [1978–79](/wiki/1978%E2%80%9379_Southern_Football_League "1978–79 Southern Football League"), they finished eighth in the league, and became founder members of the [Alliance Premier League](/wiki/Football_Conference "Football Conference"). However, they finished bottom in their [first season](/wiki/1979%E2%80%9380_Alliance_Premier_League "1979–80 Alliance Premier League"), and dropped into the Southern League's Midland Division.[[2]](#cite_note-fchdru-2)

After finishing as runners-up in [1985–86](/wiki/1985%E2%80%9386_Southern_Football_League "1985–86 Southern Football League"), the club were promoted to the Premier Division, where they remained until relegation in 1989. The following season, they reached the first round of the [FA Cup](/wiki/1989%E2%80%9390_FA_Cup "1989–90 FA Cup"), losing 3–1 to [Merthyr Tydfil](/wiki/Merthyr_Tydfil_F.C. "Merthyr Tydfil F.C."). In [2003–04](/wiki/2003%E2%80%9304_Southern_Football_League "2003–04 Southern Football League"), the club won the Southern League Western Division, and then joined [Conference North](/wiki/Conference_North "Conference North").[[2]](#cite_note-fchdru-2)

On 17 March 2011, businessman Chris Swan took over the club,[[3]](#cite_note-3) after a previous attempt to purchase Kidderminster Harriers fell through.[[4]](#cite_note-4) A short time later, in May 2011, the club was relegated from the [Conference North](/wiki/2010-11_Football_Conference "2010-11 Football Conference") after finishing bottom of the division.[[5]](#cite_note-5)

Redditch United returned to the [Southern Premier Division](/wiki/Southern_Football_League "Southern Football League") for the [2011-12](/wiki/2011-12_in_English_football "2011-12 in English football") season following their relegation the previous season. At the end of the [2015-16](/wiki/2015-16_Southern_Football_League "2015-16 Southern Football League") season, the club finished second in the division under the management of Liam McDonald, but were denied promotion after losing in the play-off semi-finals to [Leamington](/wiki/Leamington_F.C. "Leamington F.C.") on penalty kicks.[[6]](#cite_note-6)

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Redditch_United_F.C.&action=edit&section=8 "Edit section: Redditch United FC")]

* **Best [FA Trophy](/wiki/FA_Trophy "FA Trophy") performance**: 4th round, [1998–99](/wiki/1998%E2%80%9399_FA_Trophy "1998–99 FA Trophy")[[2]](#cite_note-fchdru-2)

## Honours

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Redditch_United_F.C.&action=edit&section=9 "Edit section: Honours")]

* **Birmingham Combination**
  + Champions: 1913–14, 1932–33, 1952–53
* **Birmingham & District League**
  + Southern Division champions: [1954–55](/wiki/1954%E2%80%9355_Southern_Football_League "1954–55 Southern Football League")
* **Southern League**
  + Division One North champions: [1975–76](/wiki/1975%E2%80%9376_Southern_Football_League "1975–76 Southern Football League")
  + Western Division champions: [2003–04](/wiki/2003%E2%80%9304_Southern_Football_League "2003–04 Southern Football League")
* **Birmingham Senior Cup**
  + Winners: 1925, 1932, 1939, 1977, 2005,
* **Worcestershire Senior Cup**
  + Winners: 1894, 1930, 1975, 1976, 2008, 2014
* **Staffordshire Senior Cup**
  + Winners: 1991
* **Worcester Royal Infirmary Cup**
  + Winners: 1999
